description: start.go uses an isAllCaps heuristic to bold section headers from template text (STARTING THE WORK, LANDING THE WORK), while show.go/helpers.go apply w.Style(name, Bold) directly. This works but is fragile — the styling convention is split between two mechanisms. Consider a unified approach: e.g. the template processor could support a header marker, or flush could use styleMD, or section headers could come through a callback. The goal is one mechanism for all section headers.
title: Unify section-header styling across template and show output
